100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 4, 1926, the British Parliament faced mounting pressure as debates over the controversial Trade Union Bill intensified, stirring unrest among labor groups. In the United States, President Coolidge's administration continued to grapple with economic challenges, as calls for reforms gained traction. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated with reports of increasing violence from paramilitary groups, raising concerns over stability in the Weimar Republic.
